inline_comments.message: Do you think maybe we should call "PolicyEngine.manager.getEnvironmentProperty()" in our ActorServiceProviders instead of in the drl? If we do it this current way, we will have to pass in all these values to the operation manager and not every use case needs all these values. Or we could set class variables for the ActorServiceProvider objects inside the drl if you want to keep it this way. It would be a bit messy.
Yes, this is just informational in the SETUP rule, just logs the "environment" information that this control loop is using.
The SETUP rule is supposed to be generated by policy/engine when it creates the policy. Will this rule be removed to accommodate that?

They have been removing if they need it out in the past.  I think they should not mingle with the .drl since they don't have domain expertise, and we are in the critical path to making sure it works.  Note that this is the same as in the junits drls now in R0 and in archetype for R0.   No change there.   It would really slow down our testing if we have to accommodate their UI needs.   I don't see it feasible to change it from our side because of the testing needs.
